edits.original_text: He'd spent the night smoothing wax tablets, sharpening his stylus, making copies of six years of records and placing them in trusted houses across Jerusalem. Four houses.
edits.revised_text: He'd spent the night making copies of six years of records, distributing them to four trusted houses across Jerusalem.
edits.rationale: Eliminates repetitive sentence structure and wordiness for better flow

edits.original_text: Jesus was stripped and bound to the post. The soldier took position.
edits.revised_text: They stripped Jesus and bound him to the post. The soldier took position.
edits.rationale: Uses active voice for more visceral impact

edits.original_text: Marcus's eye caught them the way it always caught things
edits.revised_text: Marcus's eye registered them the way it always captured details
edits.rationale: Uses more precise verbs to show Marcus's observational skill

edits.original_text: The boy was maybe ten... Behind her, an old woman... husband, maybe, or stranger
edits.revised_text: The boy was perhaps ten... Behind her, an old woman... husband or stranger
edits.rationale: Removes unnecessary qualifier repetition for cleaner prose

edits.original_text: Iron through flesh, through bone, through wood. The sound would follow Marcus for the rest of his life — metal on metal, then the wet sound underneath. Jesus's body arched against the wood, but he made no cry.
edits.revised_text: Iron drove through flesh, through bone, through wood—the sound of metal on metal, then the wet sound underneath that would follow Marcus for the rest of his life. Jesus's body arched against the wood, but he made no cry.
edits.rationale: Combines sentences for better rhythm and flow

edits.original_text: The same hands. The same open palms. The boy's hands raised... and now Jesus's hands opened
edits.revised_text: The same gesture. The same open palms. The boy's hands raised... and now Jesus's wrists opened
edits.rationale: Reduces repetition of 'hands' four times in one paragraph

edits.original_text: Then the sky opened. Cold rain, bitter for spring
edits.revised_text: Then the clouds split. Cold rain, bitter for spring
edits.rationale: Replaces clichéd phrase with more original imagery

edits.original_text: The steadiness of his hand was the last discipline he had. If he stopped writing, he would have to feel what he was seeing instead of recording it, and he knew — the way a man standing at the edge of a cliff knows — that if he let himself feel it, something in him would break that wouldn't go back together.
edits.revised_text: The steadiness of his hand was the last discipline he had. If he stopped writing, he would have to feel what he was seeing instead of recording it. He knew—the way a man at a cliff's edge knows—that feeling it would break something in him beyond repair.
edits.rationale: Breaks overly long sentence for better clarity and flow

edits.original_text: The memories that came now were smaller... The memories that came
edits.revised_text: The memories surfacing now were smaller... These memories arrived
edits.rationale: Uses more specific verbs to avoid repetition

edits.original_text: She waited for the third day.
edits.revised_text: She waited for the third day. But as she sat in the darkness, something in the inherited memories stirred—a warmth that had nothing to do with the present, and everything to do with what was coming.
edits.rationale: Creates stronger hook for next chapter with hint of supernatural tension
